basics.name: Junfeng Zou
basics.label: M.Phil. Student in Robotics
basics.summary: M.Phil. student in Computer and Information Engineering at The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen. Research interests include robot planning and control, surgical robotics, visual servoing, embodied learning, robot manipulation, humanoid locomotion, and real-time robotic systems.

work.company: Intelligent Healthcare & Autonomous Robotics Lab, CUHK-Shenzhen
work.summary: Vision-guided robotic nasal endoscope control and safety supervision system.
highlights: Developed a visual servoing system for robotic nasal endoscopy with Dobot CR5, integrating TCP/IP communication, ServoJ control, camera calibration, and real-time feature tracking.

Designed and validated a hierarchical MPC controller for IBVS under RCM constraints, achieving a mean image-plane tracking error of approximately 60 pixels while maintaining the RCM deviation below 1.6 mm.
Built endoscopic vision modules for instrument segmentation, distance-state estimation, and directional collision-risk assessment.

work.company: Intelligent Healthcare & Autonomous Robotics Lab, CUHK-Shenzhen
work.summary: EtherCAT-based motor control system for da Vinci surgical instruments.
highlights: Built a ROS 2, EtherCAT, and ros2_control-based multi-axis motor control framework for Denali XCR drives and MPK motors.
Implemented CiA 402 drive control, including fault reset, drive enabling, operation-mode switching, and position/velocity command execution.
Developed a motor debugging and monitoring interface for real-hardware testing, parameter tuning, and diagnosis.

work.summary: Deep reinforcement learning-based locomotion control for a humanoid robot.
highlights: Built a GPU-parallel reinforcement learning environment in Isaac Gym and implemented an Actor-Critic PPO training pipeline for 12-DoF leg control.
Designed multi-objective reward functions for velocity tracking, posture stability, gait phase regulation, and joint motion.
Trained and compared locomotion policies on flat, rough, sloped, and stair terrains.

work.company: Adaptive Robotic Controls Lab, The University of Hong Kong
work.summary: Humanoid robot assembly and EtherCAT-based joint actuation integration.
highlights: Participated in mechanical assembly and electrical integration, including joint actuator installation, motor wiring, and system commissioning.
Developed and tested an EtherCAT-based multi-joint motor control system, covering slave configuration, drive enabling, cyclic communication, and synchronized motor operation.

education.institution: The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen
education.area: M.Phil. in Computer and Information Engineering

publications.name: Flexible capacitive pressure sensor: material, structure, fabrication and application
publications.summary: C. Dong, Y. Bai, J. Zou, J. Cheng, Y. An, Z. Zhang, Z. Li, S. Lin, S. Zhao, and N. Li.

publications.name: Design and Testing of Flexible Pressure and Temperature Sensing Capacitive Sensors
publications.publisher: 2024 IEEE International Instrumentation and Measurement Technology Conference (I2MTC)
publications.summary: Y. An, J. Zou, Y. Bai, C. Dong, J. Cheng, and N. Li.

teaching.course: CSC1006: Artificial Intelligence for Science and Engineering
teaching.institution: The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen
teaching.description: Conducted tutorial sessions, answered students' questions, proctored examinations, and graded exam papers.

portfolio.name: Vision-Guided Robotic Nasal Endoscope Control and Safety Supervision System
portfolio.description: A robot-assisted nasal endoscopy project involving visual servoing, RCM-constrained MPC, endoscopic vision, and real-time safety supervision.

portfolio.name: EtherCAT-Based Motor Control System for da Vinci Surgical Instruments
portfolio.description: A ROS 2 and EtherCAT-based low-level actuation framework for Denali XCR drives, MPK motors, and surgical instrument control.

portfolio.name: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Locomotion Control for a Humanoid Robot
portfolio.description: A PPO-based locomotion control project for the open-source XBot-L humanoid robot in Isaac Gym.

portfolio.name: Humanoid Robot Assembly and EtherCAT-Based Joint Actuation Integration
portfolio.description: A visiting research project on humanoid robot assembly, electrical integration, and EtherCAT-based multi-joint motor control.

portfolio.name: LoRA-Fine-Tuned OpenVLA for Robotic Drink Pouring
portfolio.description: A real-world robotic drink-pouring VLA system based on OpenVLA-7B and LoRA.

portfolio.name: Multi-Finger Dexterous Hand Assembly and Interactive Control System
portfolio.description: A dexterous hand assembly and gesture-interaction system based on DexHand hardware, RP2350 control, MediaPipe, and OpenCV.
